action.text: const arrName: <type>[] = [xxx]; // Add the keyword readyonly to prevent it from being changed
console.log(arrName instanceof Array) // Check if it is an instance of an array

arrName.toString(); // Return a string of comma separated array values
array.join('<separator>') // Return a string with the specified separator

arrName.push(<value>); // Add a new element at the end
arrName.pop(); // Removes the last element

let pos = arrName.indexOf(<element>, <start>); // Return the position of the element (-1 if not found). 
//<start> is optional to specify the start point

let pos = arrName.lastIndexOf(<element>, <start>); // Return the last position of the element (-1 if not found). 
//<start> is optional to specify the start point

arrName.sort(); // Sort the elements alphabetically
arrName.reverse(); // Reverse the elements

let tupleName: [number, boolean, string]; // A Tuple is a typed array
tupleName = [5, false, 'text'];
